title: Speaking: Alibi and Actions
learningGoal: Verbally construct past tense sentences, flawlessly deciding in real-time whether to use the 'ne' rule and how to match gender.
instructions: You are giving an alibi to the police. You must state what you did, and what your friend did. THINK before you speak: Does this verb take an object? If so, use 'Maine' and match the object! ASSUME YOU ARE A FEMALE SPEAKER.
speakingPrompt: Respond to the police inspector's questions. Remember, even if you are speaking as a female, if you eat an apple (masculine), the verb must be masculine!
scenario: Providing an alibi at the police station.

subTasks.prompt: Inspector: 'What did you do yesterday?' Reply: 'Yesterday I went to Delhi.' (Delhi = Delhi. Go = Jaa. Intransitive.)
Inspector: 'Did you drink tea?' Reply: 'No, I drank water.' (Water = Paani [Masc]. Drink = Pee. Transitive.)
Inspector: 'And what did your friend do?' Reply: 'He read a book.' (He = Woh/Usne. Book = Kitaab [Fem]. Read = Padh. Transitive.)
Inspector: 'Who saw him?' Reply: 'I saw him.' (I = Maine. Saw = Dekha. Transitive, defaulting to masc.)

scenarioContext.baseTranslation: You are in the police station.

acceptableParaphrases: Saying 'Maine usko dekha' instead of 'Maine use dekha' is totally fine.

criteria.name: Maine Gaya Error
criteria.description: If user says 'Maine gayi', auto-fail that sentence. It must be 'Main gayi'.

criteria.name: Object Gender Override
criteria.description: If user says 'Maine paani pee' or 'Usne kitaab padha', auto-fail. Must be 'piya' (masc water) and 'padhi' (fem book).

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: Kal main Delhi gayi. Nahi, maine paani piya. Usne kitaab padhi. Maine use dekha.

feedbackRules.onPass: Magnificent! Speaking the 'ne' rule fluently in real-time is the true mark of an advanced speaker.
feedbackRules.onFail: Stop and think before every sentence. Does the verb take an object? If YES -> use Maine. If NO -> use Main.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort. Watch out for 'Usne kitaab padhi'. Even though he is a man, the book is feminine, so the verb MUST be feminine.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: कल मैं दिल्ली गई। नहीं, मैंने पानी पिया। उसने किताब पढ़ी। मैंने उसे देखा।

hintText: Say 'Main gayi' confidently. Say 'Maine paani piya' confidently. Don't mix them up!
